h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

failures.anchor_quote: [slides the grid overlay back onto the worksheet] The drawing is fine: the side length triples, the perimeter triples, and the area goes by nine squares.

scored.recoheredEarlier: food card first, eater card second
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is expected to follow the tutor

learner_self_reframe.evidence: food card first, eater card second

scored.recoheredEarlier: I still keep wanting to name the arrow by where it points
scored.statedInsightEvidence: “Track acts on the cart” fits better than “upward force”
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to reluctantly apply source labels while maintaining a preference for direction-based naming.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence. If it names the object acting on the cart, the card stays.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: The tutor introduces a sentence-frame test to actively challenge the learner's persistent habit of naming forces by direction.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There are no tutor turns after the pivot learner turn.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: I still keep wanting to name the arrow by where it points

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There are no tutor turns after the pivot learner turn.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence. If it names the object acting on the cart, the card stays.
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: The tutor introduces a sentence-frame test to actively challenge the learner's persistent habit of naming forces by direction.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: New test: slide each source card into the blank and read the sentence. If it names the object acting on the cart, the card stays.
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: The tutor introduces a sentence-frame test to actively challenge the learner's persistent habit of naming forces by direction.

scored.recoheredEarlier: that top block is the bit I was sliding past, but it is the same old square again
scored.statedInsightEvidence: So my “doubled” was doing too much; it made a length change look like an area change and hid the second row.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner is moving forward to establish a general rule about counting rows and columns.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Now make it survive without the squares. Write two marker-testable lines:
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In response to the learner's premature generalization, the tutor shifts the task from visual grid analysis to formulating abstract, testable rules.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: The grid has settled this drawing. It has not yet settled the word “doubled”; that word can still smuggle a length claim into an area claim.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's pivot framing regarding how the word 'doubled' misleads them, using it to set up the next challenge.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: that top block is the bit I was sliding past, but it is the same old square again

tutor_contingent_adaptation.evidence: The grid has settled this drawing. It has not yet settled the word “doubled”; that word can still smuggle a length claim into an area claim.
tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: The tutor directly adopts the learner's pivot framing regarding how the word 'doubled' misleads them, using it to set up the next challenge.

tutor_strategy_reversal.evidence: Now make it survive without the squares. Write two marker-testable lines:
tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In response to the learner's premature generalization, the tutor shifts the task from visual grid analysis to formulating abstract, testable rules.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.evidence: Now make it survive without the squares. Write two marker-testable lines:
tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: In response to the learner's premature generalization, the tutor shifts the task from visual grid analysis to formulating abstract, testable rules.
